attractions.name: Victoria Peak
attractions.description: The highest point on Hong Kong Island offering panoramic views of the skyline and harbor.
attractions.transport_tips: Take the Peak Tram from Garden Road.

attractions.name: Tian Tan Buddha
attractions.description: A massive bronze statue of Buddha located on Lantau Island.
attractions.transport_tips: Take the Ngong Ping 360 cable car.

attractions.name: Man Mo Temple
attractions.description: A historic temple dedicated to the gods of literature and war, famous for its giant incense coils.
attractions.transport_tips: Located in Sheung Wan, accessible by MTR.

attractions.name: Star Ferry
attractions.description: Iconic ferry service crossing Victoria Harbour between Tsim Sha Tsui and Central.
attractions.transport_tips: Use the Octopus card for payment.

attractions.name: Hong Kong Museum of History
attractions.description: Comprehensive exhibits covering the history and development of Hong Kong.
attractions.transport_tips: Short walk from Tsim Sha Tsui MTR station.
attractions.address: 100 Chatham Rd S, Tsim Sha Tsui

attractions.name: Ocean Park
attractions.description: A marine-themed amusement park with rides and animal exhibits.
attractions.transport_tips: Take the MTR South Island Line to Ocean Park Station.

attractions.name: Dragon's Back
attractions.description: A popular hiking trail offering stunning coastal views of the South China Sea.
attractions.transport_tips: Take bus 9 from Shau Kei Wan MTR.

attractions.name: Hong Kong Disneyland
attractions.description: The Disney theme park located on Lantau Island.
attractions.transport_tips: Take the MTR Disneyland Resort Line.

restaurants.name: Tim Ho Wan
restaurants.description: Famous Michelin-starred dim sum restaurant known for its baked BBQ pork buns.

restaurants.name: Mak's Noodle
restaurants.description: Renowned for traditional wonton noodles in a small, authentic setting.

restaurants.name: Lung King Heen
restaurants.description: High-end Cantonese dining at the Four Seasons Hotel with harbor views.

restaurants.name: Yat Lok Roast Goose
restaurants.description: Famous for its crispy-skinned roast goose and traditional Hong Kong style meats.

hidden_gems.name: Ping Shan Heritage Trail
hidden_gems.description: A walk through historic walled villages and ancestral halls in the New Territories.

nightlife.name: Lan Kwai Fong
nightlife.description: The premier nightlife district in Central, packed with bars, clubs, and restaurants.

shopping.name: Temple Street Night Market
shopping.description: A bustling street market in Yau Ma Tei featuring souvenirs, clothes, and street food.

festivals.name: Lunar New Year
festivals.description: The biggest festival in Hong Kong with fireworks and parades.

adventure_activities.name: Dragon's Back Hike
adventure_activities.description: A scenic ridge hike overlooking the coast.

local_tips: Get an Octopus card for all public transport and convenience store payments.
Tipping is not mandatory but appreciated in high-end restaurants.
Always carry an umbrella; weather can change quickly.
Learn basic Cantonese phrases like 'm-goi' (thank you/excuse me).

safety.general: Hong Kong is very safe, but keep an eye on belongings in crowded areas.
safety.water_safety: Tap water is generally safe to drink, but many locals boil it first.
safety.transport_safety: Public transport is extremely safe and reliable.

culture.overview: A unique blend of traditional Chinese culture and British colonial history.
culture.etiquette: Respect elders, avoid pointing fingers, and keep noise levels low in public transport.

transport.getting_around: Use the MTR (subway), iconic double-decker buses, and the Star Ferry.
transport.airport: Hong Kong International Airport (HKG) is connected to the city by the Airport Express train.

visa_information.domestic_indian_travellers: Indian nationals must complete a Pre-arrival Registration (PAR) online before traveling.
visa_information.foreign_nationals: Many nationalities enjoy visa-free entry for up to 90 days; check official immigration websites.

weather.best_time_to_visit: October to December (cool, dry, and sunny).
weather.avoid: July to September (typhoon season and high humidity).
